name: Company Expenses API
description: REST API for corporate expense reporting: employees submit claims with receipts, managers approve or reject, finance processes reimbursements, and admins configure company settings—with JWT security, Flyway migrations, and Docker deploy.

problemStatement.problemDescription: Companies often track employee expenses in spreadsheets or email threads. Managers lack a single queue for pending approvals, finance cannot tie reimbursements to approved claims, and employees have no audit trail for receipts or rejection reasons.
problemList: Expense status scattered across email and spreadsheets
No role-separated API for employees vs managers vs finance vs admins
Receipt attachments stored ad hoc without linkage to expense records
Managers need paginated filters by status and monthly summaries
No standardized JSON envelope for frontend clients

solution.solutionTitle: A Spring Boot API for the full expense lifecycle

solutionList.title: JWT auth with role-based routes
solutionList.description: Register employees and managers publicly; admins register other admins. OAuth2 resource server validates Bearer tokens with roles claim mapped to Spring authorities.

solutionList.title: Employee expense submission
solutionList.description: Authenticated employees create PENDING expenses, upload multipart attachments, and list their own claims via JWT subject email.

solutionList.title: Manager approval workflow
solutionList.description: Managers and admins approve or reject expenses, soft-delete records, filter by status with sorting, and fetch date-range summaries.

solutionList.title: Finance reimbursements
solutionList.description: MANAGER or FINANCE roles create reimbursements linked to expenses; notifications fire on state changes.

solutionList.title: Admin settings & dashboard
solutionList.description: Admin-only dashboard stats and mutable company settings stored in the database.

solutionList.title: Containerized local and cloud deploy
solutionList.description: Multi-stage Docker image, local compose with PostgreSQL 15, production compose with external DB host from `.env`.

metricsList: ~75 main Java source files + 11 test classes
2 Flyway SQL migrations (schema + demo seed data)
API served on port 8080; Swagger UI at `/swagger-ui.html`
Global Bucket4j interceptor on `/**` (20 token bucket, 10 refill/min)

mediaGallery.title: Company Expenses API — product views
mediaGallery.description: Placeholder assets for portfolio UI. Replace with Swagger screenshots, admin dashboard mockups, or sequence diagrams from your frontend.

items.title: OpenAPI documentation
items.description: springdoc Swagger UI for interactive endpoint exploration

layers.name: Presentation (clients)
layers.description: Web or mobile clients for employees, managers, finance, and admins.
responsibilities: Render ResponseWrapper success and error states

layers.name: API & security
layers.description: Spring MVC controllers, validation, JWT resource server, and global rate limiting.

layers.name: Application services
layers.description: Business logic, notifications, email, and file uploads.

layers.name: Persistence
layers.description: JPA entities, repositories, and Flyway-managed PostgreSQL schema.

designPatterns.title: Layered architecture
designPatterns.description: Controllers delegate to services; services use repositories and mappers—classic Spring layering.

designPatterns.title: DTO + Mapper
designPatterns.description: MapStruct mappers translate entities to API DTOs, keeping persistence models out of HTTP contracts.

designPatterns.title: Result object
designPatterns.description: Result<T> wraps success/failure for auth validation without exceptions for expected failures.

designPatterns.title: Response wrapper
designPatterns.description: ResponseWrapper factory methods standardize success, created, notFound, and badRequest payloads.

designPatterns.title: Repository
designPatterns.description: Spring Data JPA repositories encapsulate queries and pagination.

designPatterns.title: Interceptor — rate limit
designPatterns.description: HandlerInterceptor consumes Bucket4j tokens before controller execution.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API instances
scalabilityStrategies.description: JWT-backed auth allows horizontal scaling of Spring Boot containers behind a load balancer.

scalabilityStrategies.title: External PostgreSQL
scalabilityStrategies.description: Database lives outside the app container in production compose—scale app tier independently.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Paginated list endpoints
scalabilityStrategies.description: Manager and employee list endpoints use Spring Data Pageable to limit payload size.

scalabilityStrategies.title: File storage externalization (future)
scalabilityStrategies.description: Move FILE_UPLOAD_DIR to S3-compatible object storage for multi-instance deploys.

securityStrategies.title: JWT resource server
securityStrategies.description: Bearer tokens validated per request; roles claim drives hasRole checks.

securityStrategies.title: Path-based authorization
securityStrategies.description: Separate prefixes for admin, manager, employee, and reimbursement routes.

securityStrategies.title: Password hashing
securityStrategies.description: PasswordHandler for credential storage (see UserService / auth flow).

securityStrategies.title: Global rate limiting
securityStrategies.description: Mitigates brute-force and abuse via Bucket4j 429 responses.

securityStrategies.title: CSRF disabled
securityStrategies.description: Appropriate for pure JSON API clients; ensure HTTPS-only in production.

cacheStrategies.name: Caffeine (Spring Cache)
cacheStrategies.description: spring-boot-starter-cache with Caffeine on classpath—ready for @Cacheable on hot reads.
cacheStrategies.coverage: Not widely applied in controllers yet

cacheStrategies.name: No Redis in current deploy
cacheStrategies.description: Unlike Django template docs, this project does not bundle Redis; rate limits are in-process Bucket4j.

architectureFeatures.title: Unified API envelope
architectureFeatures.description: ResponseWrapper provides consistent JSON for clients and tests.

architectureFeatures.title: Role-separated API surface
architectureFeatures.description: Distinct controller packages/paths per actor: employee, manager, admin, finance.

requestFlow.title: Client request
requestFlow.description: Client calls REST endpoint with JSON body and optional Bearer JWT.

requestFlow.title: Rate limit & security
requestFlow.description: RateLimitInterceptor consumes token; SecurityFilterChain validates JWT and roles.

requestFlow.title: Controller → service
requestFlow.description: Controller validates DTOs, delegates to @Service (e.g. ExpenseService.createExpense).

requestFlow.title: Persistence
requestFlow.description: JPA repositories write/read PostgreSQL; Flyway ensures schema version.

requestFlow.title: Response
requestFlow.description: ResponseWrapper JSON returned; notifications/email may fire asynchronously in-thread.

eventFlow.title: Expense state change
eventFlow.description: Submit, approve, or reject updates expense row and may create notification row.

eventFlow.title: NotificationService
eventFlow.description: builds in-app notification for affected user.

eventFlow.title: Email (optional)
eventFlow.description: EmailService can send templated mail when SMTP configured.

eventFlow.title: Reimbursement
eventFlow.description: Finance creates reimbursement record linked to expense.

techDecisions.title: Spring Boot monolith
techDecisions.problem: Small team needs one deployable unit with clear modules, not microservice overhead.
techDecisions.solution: Single Gradle project with controller/service/repository packages.

techDecisions.title: PostgreSQL + Flyway
techDecisions.problem: Relational expense data with FK integrity and audit fields.
techDecisions.solution: PostgreSQL with versioned SQL migrations instead of ddl-auto in prod.
techDecisions.outcome: Predictable schema; demo seed in V2 migration.

techDecisions.title: JWT (OAuth2 resource server)
techDecisions.problem: SPA/mobile clients need stateless auth across instances.
techDecisions.solution: Encode roles in JWT; validate with JwtDecoder per request.
techDecisions.outcome: Works with horizontal scaling; no refresh token flow yet.

techDecisions.title: In-process rate limiting
techDecisions.problem: Basic abuse protection without Redis infrastructure.
techDecisions.solution: Bucket4j bucket in RateLimitInterceptor for all paths.
techDecisions.outcome: Simple for single instance; per-instance counters when scaled out.

codeExamples.title: JWT issuance with role claims
codeExamples.description: JWTService encodes subject (email) and roles list for Spring Security resource server consumption.

files.name: JWTService.java
files.explanation: Tokens include issuer, expiry, subject, and roles claim stripped of ROLE_ prefix in authorities mapping.

codeExamples.title: Path-based security rules
codeExamples.description: SecurityConfig declares public auth/swagger routes and role requirements per API prefix.

files.name: SecurityConfig.java
files.explanation: Note FINANCIAL vs Role.FINANCE mismatch for reimbursement routes.

codeExamples.title: Standard API response envelope
codeExamples.description: ResponseWrapper factory methods keep HTTP payloads consistent for clients.

files.name: ResponseWrapper.java
files.explanation: Fields: success, data, message, code, time_stamp.

codeExamples.title: Global Bucket4j rate limiting
codeExamples.description: Every request consumes one token; exhaustion returns 429 JSON.

files.name: RateLimitInterceptor.java

codeExamples.title: Submit expense with validation
codeExamples.description: EmployeeExpenseController validates DTO, creates PENDING expense, notifies stakeholders.

files.name: EmployeeExpenseController.java
files.explanation: Email taken from JWT via JWTService.getEmailFromRequest.

features.title: JWT authentication & role-based access
features.description: OAuth2 resource server with JWT decoder; roles claim mapped to Spring authorities (ROLE_EMPLOYEE, ROLE_MANAGER, ROLE_ADMIN, ROLE_FINANCE). Auth endpoints issue tokens on register/login.
highlights: Stateless session; CSRF disabled for API usage

features.title: Expense submission & approval
features.description: Employees create PENDING expenses; managers approve, reject with reason, or soft-delete; status transitions trigger notifications.
highlights: Categories: TRAVEL, FOOD, OFFICE_SUPPLIES, SOFTWARE_LICENSES, ENTERTAINMENT
Statuses: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, REIMBURSED, MISCELLANEOUS
Paginated manager views by status and user

features.title: Receipt attachments
features.description: Multipart uploads stored via FileHandler to configurable FILE_UPLOAD_DIR; metadata in expense_attachments table.

features.title: Reimbursement processing
features.description: Finance/manager roles record reimbursements against approved expenses with processed_by audit.

features.title: In-app notifications & email
features.description: Notification entities for user events; EmailService + Thymeleaf templates for outbound mail when configured.

features.title: Global API rate limiting
features.description: Bucket4j classic bucket applied to all routes through WebMvc interceptor; returns 429 with Retry-After style header.
highlights: JSON error body on quota exhaustion

features.title: Database migrations
features.description: Flyway manages PostgreSQL schema; V1 creates users, expenses, attachments, reimbursements, notifications; V2 seeds demo data.
highlights: CHECK constraints on category and status

features.title: OpenAPI & Swagger UI
features.description: springdoc-openapi exposes /api-docs and /swagger-ui.html for interactive testing.
highlights: OpenApiConfig metadata (title Expense Tracking API v1.0)

features.title: Docker & Compose profiles
features.description: Gradle multi-stage build producing boot JAR; local compose runs app + Postgres; prod compose runs app only against external DB.
highlights: docker/Dockerfile — JDK 17 build, Temurin 17 JRE runtime

metrics.label: API port
metrics.description: server.port in application.properties; exposed in Docker compose

metrics.label: Local Postgres host port
metrics.description: Maps container 5432 for host access during Gradle bootRun

metrics.label: Java runtime
metrics.description: Eclipse Temurin JRE in production image

cloudServices.name: PostgreSQL (managed or self-hosted)
cloudServices.purpose: Primary datastore for users, expenses, reimbursements, notifications, admin settings

cloudServices.name: SMTP provider
cloudServices.cost: Variable / free tier on many providers

cloudServices.name: Railway / Render / Fly.io (optional)
cloudServices.purpose: PaaS deploy using docker/Dockerfile and injected env vars per docker/README.md

cloudServices.name: Local filesystem

cloudServices.name: GitHub Actions (placeholder)

components.name: Employee web / mobile app
components.description: Submits expenses and uploads receipts (future or separate frontend)

components.name: Bucket4j interceptor
components.description: Global rate limit on incoming HTTP

components.name: PostgreSQL 15
components.description: Local container in compose.local; external host in prod

dockerFiles.description: Local development: Spring Boot app + PostgreSQL 15 with healthcheck and named volume.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    environment:
      DB_HOST: db
      DB_PORT: "5432"
  db:
    image: postgres:15
    ports:
      - "5431:5432"
volumes:
  postgres_data:

dockerFiles.description: Production profile: app container only; DB_HOST points to external Postgres.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  app:
    build:
      context: ..
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    env_file:
      - ../.env
    restart: unless-stopped

dockerFiles.description: Multi-stage: Gradle 8.5 JDK 17 build (skip tests), Temurin 17 JRE runs app.jar.
dockerFiles.content:
FROM gradle:8.5-jdk17 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN gradle build -x test
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy
COPY --from=build /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

httpEndpoints.description: Interactive OpenAPI documentation (springdoc). Also available under /swagger-ui/** and /v3/api-docs/**.
Machine-readable API description (springdoc.api-docs.path).
Validates email/password and returns a JWT string in ResponseWrapper.data.
Creates a user with role EMPLOYEE and returns JWT on success.
Creates a user with role MANAGER and returns JWT.
Requires authenticated ADMIN (`@PreAuthorize`). Creates another ADMIN user.
Resolves user from JWT subject (email) via JWTService.
Uses JWT email from request; path userId is present in mapping but listing is scoped by token subject.
Creates expense in PENDING status for authenticated employee; triggers notification.
Multipart file upload; stores file via FileHandler and links Attachment entity.

Paginated expenses for a user. Note: path variable is userId but handler binds email — verify client usage.
Filters by ExpenseStatus; defaults to PENDING if status unknown. Sorts by createdAt ASC/DESC.
Returns ExpenseSummary for optional startDate/endDate; defaults to current calendar month.
Sets approved status; records approver email from JWT. Mapping in code may be missing slash before {expenseId} — clients should test actual path.
Requires ExpenseRejectDTO body with expenseId and rejection reason.
Sets deleted_at on expense record.
Requires MANAGER or FINANCE role (see SecurityConfig vs Role enum alignment).
Single reimbursement lookup.
Links reimbursement to expense; processed_by from JWT email.
Authenticated users (any role with valid JWT).
Paginated notifications.
Manual notification insert (also used internally from expense events).
Updates read flag on notification.
Returns AdminDashboardDTO (stats). ADMIN role only.
Current SettingsDTO from AdminService.
Persists SettingsDTO.
